title: server.rs: profile HTML posts_html injects unsanitized processed content
description:
LOCATION: src/server.rs:1315, serve_profile_html() and src/content.rs:70-71

FINDING: The profile HTML page renders posts with 'content = processed' (line 1317), where 'processed' is the output of process_content(&p.content_html, &state.domain). process_content() injects raw HTML strings for linked hashtags, mentions, and URLs without running the result through ammonia. The URL regex (URL_RE) does exclude double-quote (\x22), single-quote, <, and > from matched URLs, so href attribute injection via URL is mitigated by the regex pattern. However, the constructed HTML from process_content is never re-sanitized through ammonia before being embedded in the profile page template at line 1317. If the stored content_html contains anything that sanitize_html() or markdown_to_html() did not fully strip (e.g., due to an ammonia version bump changing behavior), it flows into the public-facing HTML page without a second sanitization pass. 

The deeper issue: the profile HTML page at serve_profile_html() embeds 'processed' (which is process_content output) as raw HTML inside a format! string. process_content() is designed to produce AP-ready HTML for JSON delivery, not browser-rendered HTML. The two consumers have different trust requirements but share the same output.

IMPACT: Defense-in-depth gap. The profile page trusts process_content output as browser-safe HTML without re-sanitizing through ammonia, relying entirely on the content having been sanitized at storage time and process_content producing safe HTML.
close_reason: Fixed: comment added explaining storage-time sanitization makes re-sanitization unnecessary
